moon_deities.name: Sin
moon_deities.description: Sin, also known as Nanna, is the Mesopotamian god of the moon, associated with wisdom and the calendar.

moon_deities.name: Nanna
moon_deities.description: Nanna is the Sumerian god of the moon, father of Utu (sun god) and Inanna (goddess of love and war).

moon_deities.name: Khonsu
moon_deities.description: Khonsu is the Egyptian god of the moon, time, and healing, often depicted with a sidelock of youth and a lunar disk.

moon_deities.name: Iah
moon_deities.description: Iah is an ancient Egyptian lunar deity, sometimes merged with Thoth and Khonsu.

moon_deities.name: Selene
moon_deities.description: Selene is the Greek goddess of the Moon, often depicted driving a silver chariot across the night sky.

moon_deities.name: Artemis
moon_deities.description: Artemis is the goddess of the hunt, wilderness, and the moon, associated with chastity and protection.

moon_deities.name: Hecate
moon_deities.description: Hecate is a Greek goddess associated with magic, witchcraft, the night, and the waning moon.

moon_deities.name: Luna
moon_deities.description: Luna is the Roman goddess of the moon, equivalent to the Greek Selene.

moon_deities.name: Diana
moon_deities.description: Diana is the Roman goddess of the hunt and the moon, protector of women and children.

moon_deities.name: Chandra
moon_deities.description: Chandra is the Hindu moon god, associated with the night, vegetation, and fertility.

moon_deities.name: Soma
moon_deities.description: Soma is the Vedic deity of the moon and the personification of the sacred soma plant.

moon_deities.name: Tsukuyomi
moon_deities.description: Tsukuyomi is the Japanese moon god, born from Izanagi, representing order and night.

moon_deities.name: Chang'e
moon_deities.description: Chang'e is the Chinese moon goddess who lives on the moon after consuming an elixir of immortality.

moon_deities.name: Yue Lao
moon_deities.description: Yue Lao is the Chinese god of marriage and love, associated with the moon and red thread of fate.

moon_deities.name: Coyolxauhqui
moon_deities.description: Coyolxauhqui is the Aztec moon goddess, depicted as a dismembered warrior, symbolizing the moon's phases.

moon_deities.name: Tecciztecatl
moon_deities.description: Tecciztecatl is the Aztec moon god, associated with the creation of the moon and bravery.

moon_deities.name: Ix Chel
moon_deities.description: Ix Chel is the Mayan goddess of the moon, fertility, medicine, and weaving.

moon_deities.name: Mama Quilla
moon_deities.description: Mama Quilla is the Incan goddess of the moon and marriage, protector of women.

moon_deities.name: Máni
moon_deities.description: Máni is the Norse personification of the moon, who guides the moon across the sky in a chariot.

moon_deities.name: Cerridwen
moon_deities.description: Cerridwen is a Welsh goddess of the moon, wisdom, and transformation, keeper of the cauldron of knowledge.

moon_deities.name: Rhiannon
moon_deities.description: Rhiannon is a Welsh goddess associated with the moon, horses, and the Otherworld.

moon_deities.name: Ningal
moon_deities.description: Ningal is the Sumerian goddess of the moon and consort of the moon god Nanna.

moon_deities.name: Thoth
moon_deities.description: Thoth is the Egyptian god of wisdom and the moon, often depicted with the head of an ibis.

moon_deities.name: Hathor
moon_deities.description: Hathor, primarily a sky goddess, also had lunar associations and was linked to the cycles of the moon.

moon_deities.name: Parvati
moon_deities.description: Parvati, consort of Shiva, is associated with the moon and represents fertility and devotion.

moon_deities.name: Budha
moon_deities.description: Budha is the Hindu deity of the planet Mercury, son of Chandra (moon god), and associated with wisdom.

moon_deities.name: Houyi
moon_deities.description: Houyi is a Chinese archer hero who shot down nine suns and is associated with the moon through his wife Chang'e.

moon_deities.name: Wu Gang
moon_deities.description: Wu Gang is a mythical figure condemned to cut a self-healing tree on the moon for eternity.

moon_deities.name: Amaterasu
moon_deities.description: Amaterasu is primarily the sun goddess in Shinto, but has lunar aspects in some regional beliefs.

moon_deities.name: Metztli
moon_deities.description: Metztli is a Mesoamerican moon deity, sometimes considered male or female, associated with night and time.

moon_deities.name: Awilix
moon_deities.description: Awilix is a Mayan moon and night goddess, associated with the moon, war, and the underworld.

moon_deities.name: Viracocha
moon_deities.description: Viracocha is the Incan creator god with associations to the moon and celestial order.

moon_deities.name: Sól
moon_deities.description: Sól is the Norse sun goddess, often contrasted with her brother Máni, the moon god.

moon_deities.name: Arianrhod
moon_deities.description: Arianrhod is a Welsh moon goddess associated with fertility, rebirth, and the stars.

moon_deities.name: Elen of the Ways
moon_deities.description: Elen of the Ways is a mysterious Celtic goddess associated with moonlit paths and ley lines.

moon_deities.name: Mawu
moon_deities.description: Mawu is the West African goddess of the moon, night, and fertility, often paired with Lisa, the sun god.

moon_deities.name: Gleti
moon_deities.description: Gleti is the Ewe moon goddess, considered the mother of all stars.

moon_deities.name: iNyanga
moon_deities.description: iNyanga is the Zulu moon spirit, associated with healing and timekeeping.

moon_deities.name: Hina
moon_deities.description: Hina is a Polynesian moon goddess, often associated with creativity, tapa cloth, and tides.

moon_deities.name: Mahina
moon_deities.description: Mahina is the Tahitian goddess of the moon, associated with femininity and time.

moon_deities.name: Myesyats
moon_deities.description: Myesyats is the Slavic personification of the moon, often depicted as a male deity associated with time and the calendar.

moon_deities.name: Mēness
moon_deities.description: Mēness is the Latvian god of the moon, associated with war and the guardian of warriors.

moon_deities.name: Marama
moon_deities.description: Marama is the Maori god of the moon, associated with the lunar calendar and tides.

moon_deities.name: Alignak
moon_deities.description: Alignak is the Inuit god of the moon, weather, and tides, often associated with the sea and sky.

moon_deities.name: Hanwi
moon_deities.description: Hanwi is the Lakota moon goddess, guardian of the night and protector of women.

moon_deities.name: Deba
moon_deities.description: Deba is a Cherokee moon spirit associated with fertility and the agricultural cycle.

moon_deities.name: Tlunghu
moon_deities.description: Tlunghu is a Haida moon spirit who governs the tides and is associated with transformation.

moon_deities.name: Tsyolkʼal
moon_deities.description: Tsyolkʼal is a Mayan lunar calendar spirit, representing the sacred 260-day cycle.

moon_deities.name: Ay Ata
moon_deities.description: Ay Ata is the Turkic moon god, considered the father of the moon and a symbol of wisdom.

moon_deities.name: Mah
moon_deities.description: Mah is the Persian moon deity, associated with timekeeping and the calendar.

moon_deities.name: Al-Qamar
moon_deities.description: Al-Qamar is the personification of the moon in pre-Islamic Arabian mythology.

moon_deities.name: Tagaloa-lagi
moon_deities.description: Tagaloa-lagi is a Samoan sky god with lunar aspects, associated with creation and celestial cycles.

moon_deities.name: Anningan
moon_deities.description: Anningan is the Inuit moon god who chases his sister, the sun, across the sky.

moon_deities.name: Wuriupranili
moon_deities.description: Wuriupranili is an Aboriginal solar goddess with lunar associations, traveling across the sky with a torch.

moon_deities.name: Jacheongbi
moon_deities.description: Jacheongbi is a Korean goddess of agriculture and the moon, known for her wisdom and resilience.

moon_deities.name: Chup Kamui
moon_deities.description: Chup Kamui is the Ainu goddess of the moon, revered as a protector and guide during the night.

moon_deities.name: Mawu-Lisa
moon_deities.description: Mawu-Lisa is a dual deity representing the moon (Mawu) and sun (Lisa), symbolizing balance and cosmic order.

moon_deities.name: Nokomis
moon_deities.description: Nokomis is the Ojibwe moon grandmother spirit, a wise elder and nurturer in tribal stories.

moon_deities.name: Ayla
moon_deities.description: Ayla is a Turkish moon goddess whose name means 'halo of light around the moon'.

moon_deities.name: Tsagaan Eej
moon_deities.description: Tsagaan Eej, or the White Old Mother, is a Mongolian goddess associated with the moon, fertility, and protection.

moon_deities.name: Bai Ulgan
moon_deities.description: Bai Ulgan is a creator god in Altai mythology with associations to the moon and sky.

moon_deities.name: Osupa
moon_deities.description: Osupa is the Yoruba moon goddess, associated with night, femininity, and cycles.

moon_deities.name: Bia
moon_deities.description: Bia is an Akan deity associated with the moon, wilderness, and the night.

moon_deities.name: Nommo
moon_deities.description: Nommo are ancestral spirits in Dogon mythology, associated with water, the moon, and order.

moon_deities.name: Tane Mahuta
moon_deities.description: Tane Mahuta is a forest god with lunar associations, linked to the rhythms of nature and time.

moon_deities.name: Sina
moon_deities.description: Sina is a Samoan goddess associated with the moon and the origin of the coconut tree.

moon_deities.name: Nokomea
moon_deities.description: Nokomea is a Tongan moon goddess associated with timekeeping and fertility.

moon_deities.name: Lewanavanua
moon_deities.description: Lewanavanua is a Fijian sky deity with lunar aspects, associated with navigation and tides.

moon_deities.name: Arasy
moon_deities.description: Arasy is the Guarani goddess of the moon and love, residing in the sky and watching over the earth.

moon_deities.name: Jacy
moon_deities.description: Jacy is the Tupi moon goddess, associated with love, protection, and the night sky.

moon_deities.name: Kokyangwuti
moon_deities.description: Kokyangwuti, or Spider Grandmother, is a Hopi deity associated with the moon, wisdom, and creation.

moon_deities.name: Pawnee Moon Spirit
moon_deities.description: The Pawnee Moon Spirit is revered as a guide and protector, influencing agriculture and time.

moon_deities.name: Nokomis (Cree)
moon_deities.description: Nokomis is the Cree moon grandmother spirit, a wise elder who guides the people and controls the tides.

moon_deities.name: Tecuhtli
moon_deities.description: Tecuhtli is an Aztec moon god associated with nobility, night, and the lunar calendar.

moon_deities.name: Yolkai Estsan
moon_deities.description: Yolkai Estsan is the Navajo goddess of the moon and fertility, associated with the white shell and water.

moon_deities.name: Mapu
moon_deities.description: Mapu is a Mapuche lunar deity representing the earth and moon, central to agricultural cycles.

moon_deities.name: Fati
moon_deities.description: Fati is a Tuvaluan moon god, son of the creator god and associated with night and navigation.

moon_deities.name: Avatea
moon_deities.description: Avatea is a lunar deity of the Cook Islands, associated with light and the duality of day and night.

moon_deities.name: Selu
moon_deities.description: Selu is the Cherokee goddess of corn and the moon, symbolizing sustenance and cycles.

moon_deities.name: Meztli
moon_deities.description: Meztli is another name for the Aztec moon deity, often associated with night, dreams, and time.

moon_deities.name: Chandra-Ma
moon_deities.description: Chandra-Ma is a Buddhist lunar deity representing serenity, meditation, and the passage of time.

moon_deities.name: Phoebe
moon_deities.description: Phoebe is a Titaness associated with the moon and prophecy, grandmother of Apollo and Artemis.

moon_deities.name: Raka
moon_deities.description: Raka is the Maori god of winds with lunar associations, governing tides and weather patterns.

moon_deities.name: Hina-uri
moon_deities.description: Hina-uri is a Hawaiian moon goddess, associated with transformation and the night.

moon_deities.name: Mēn
moon_deities.description: Mēn is a Phrygian moon god depicted with a crescent and associated with time and fate.

moon_deities.name: Khonshu
moon_deities.description: Khonshu is a form of Khonsu, associated with the new moon and protection during travel at night.
